us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, War Crimes Questions Brushed Off
us-politics.body:
Trump held a news conference doubling down on a Tuesday deadline for Iran to accept a nuclear deal, warning the country 'could be taken out in one night' and floating the idea of charging tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage. He brushed aside questions about whether strikes on civilian infrastructure would constitute war crimes. Iran rejected a ceasefire proposal, with a former NATO commander noting the regime still holds leverage.

The administration separately detailed a rescue operation for a missing F-15 airman in Iran, which Defense Secretary Hegseth framed in religious terms. Former national security adviser John Bolton argued that extending the deadline signals to Tehran that Trump is not serious. OPEC+ announced plans to boost oil output once the Strait reopens, suggesting markets are already pricing in a potential resolution.

sources.title: 5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Former NATO commander on Iran rejecting ceasefire proposal: ‘They still have cards to play’
Live updates: Trump warns all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’ as he sticks to deadline
Bolton: Trump extending deadline shows Iran ‘he’s not really serious’
Hegseth ties Iran rescue to Easter story and Jesus Christ: 'A pilot reborn'
Former CIA director: ‘Russia no longer has the upper hand’ in Ukraine war
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Backs Bannon; Trump Attacks Court Over Rulings
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon in his bid to dismiss his January 6 contempt conviction, a significant legal win for one of Trump's closest allies. The ruling comes as Trump publicly attacked the Court over separate decisions on birthright citizenship and tariffs, accusing the justices of overreach.

The tension between the executive branch and the judiciary is sharpening on multiple fronts. Trump's criticism of the Court is unusual in its directness and reflects a broader pattern of the administration testing institutional limits.

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs

us-politics.headline: GOP Eyes 2026 With Super PAC Cash and Senate Picks
us-politics.body:
Republicans are moving early on the 2026 midterms. A GOP super PAC announced a $342 million investment in Senate races, while a separate AI-backed super PAC claimed wins in early GOP primaries and is expanding into more contests. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor, aiming to hold the seat.

Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin told Sean Hannity he has 'more to give,' fueling speculation about his political ambitions beyond the statehouse. The early money and candidate activity signal Republicans are treating the midterm map as competitive and worth defending aggressively.

sources.title: AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’

us-politics.headline: Education, EPA and Military Shake-Ups Signal Policy Shift
us-politics.body:
The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity, continuing the administration's rollback of Biden-era policies. The EPA took an early step toward unwinding methane flaring rules, drawing criticism from environmental groups and quiet support from energy producers.

At the Pentagon, Hegseth cut ties with Ivy League institutions in military education programs and directed funding toward Hillsdale College, a conservative school with no federal funding. Separately, an NPR report found that proposed changes to the H-2A agricultural visa program are drawing opposition from both conservative farm groups and liberal immigration advocates, a rare point of cross-partisan friction.

sources.title: Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers

au-politics.headline: Trump Slams Australia Over Iran as Conflict Escalates
au-politics.body: Donald Trump has publicly criticised Australia for failing to support US action against Iran, saying the country 'didn't help' as Washington threatens what he called the 'demolition' of Tehran. Iran has rejected a truce deal, and the conflict is now rippling through Australian supply chains - PVC prices are spiking, hitting the construction and plumbing sectors hard. Fuel supply is also under pressure, with the federal government ruling out rationing for now. Communications Minister Michelle Rowland says the focus remains on securing supply. The tension puts Canberra in a difficult position, balancing the alliance with Washington against the economic fallout of a widening Middle East war.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; ‘increasing’ cyclone threat for Queensland
Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket
Live: Government focused on supply, 'not considering' fuel rationing: Rowland
Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia
Morning Mail: ‘scam’ empire links alleged in Timor-Leste, Trump says Iran could be ‘taken out tomorrow night’
Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Iran rejects truce deal

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Queensland as Reef Damage Mounts
au-politics.body: Tropical Cyclone Maila is forecast to cross Far North Queensland, adding to a string of recent weather events battering the state's coastline and ecosystems. At Ningaloo Reef in Western Australia, scientists fear a recent cyclone wiped out coral spawning, dealing what researchers describe as a double blow to an already stressed system. On the Great Barrier Reef, wrecked boats have been leaking styrofoam and chemicals for months, with salvage timelines drawing sharp criticism from conservationists and local operators. The back-to-back incidents are intensifying debate about how quickly authorities respond to environmental threats in marine protected areas.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; ‘increasing’ cyclone threat for Queensland
'Double whammy' for Ningaloo Reef amid fears cyclone wiped out coral spawning
Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months
Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

au-politics.headline: Renewable Energy Policy Faces Scrutiny Over Farmers and Conflicts
au-politics.body: Farmers are pushing for stronger protections in Australia's renewable energy policy, arguing they are being left exposed as large-scale wind and solar projects expand across agricultural land. Separately, a planned Tasmanian wind farm has drawn attention because of its links to state minister Bridget Archer, with some local residents raising concerns about the approval process. The two stories together highlight a growing tension in the energy transition - between the pace of rollout demanded by climate targets and the community and governance questions that keep surfacing around individual projects. Neither the government nor its critics have resolved how to balance those competing pressures.

sources.title: Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy policy
Planned wind farm linked to Tasmanian minister has some residents worried

au-politics.headline: Army Reservist Charged Over Alleged Unauthorised Ukraine Service
au-politics.body: A South Australian army reservist has been charged after allegedly serving with Ukrainian military forces without authorisation from the Australian Defence Force. The case is the first of its kind to receive significant public attention and raises questions about how Australia manages reservists who seek to join foreign conflicts, particularly in a war that Australia officially supports through aid and equipment. Defence law prohibits serving in foreign militaries without approval, regardless of the cause. The case is likely to prompt scrutiny of whether current rules are clear enough - and whether enforcement has kept pace with the number of Australians drawn to the conflict in Ukraine.

sources.title: Army reservist allegedly worked for Ukraine military without authorisation

world.headline: US-Israel Strike Iran as Deadline Looms, War Widens
world.body:
The United States and Israel are actively bombing Iran, hitting targets including the Bushehr nuclear power plant, a top university, and residential areas where rescuers are still pulling survivors from rubble. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed strikes are intensifying as Trump's deadline for Iran to surrender control of the Strait of Hormuz approaches, with Trump threatening to destroy Iran in a single night if no deal is reached.

Iran is pushing a counter-proposal to end the war, but there is little sign Washington is receptive. Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in striking Israel, broadening the conflict across the region. Trump separately claimed Iranians are begging the US to continue bombing - and threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of a downed US airman rescued from Iranian territory.

sources.title: Trump's deadline nears - with little indication Iran is on board
Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
Why is Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant being attacked?
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Burn as Israeli Offensive Deepens
world.body:
Israeli air strikes killed at least ten Palestinians near a school in Gaza, with additional deaths reported from clashes between Hamas and a militia in the territory. Israeli forces also conducted raids in Hebron, firing tear gas at Palestinian youth. The violence is not contained to Gaza.

Israel has intensified its ground offensive in Lebanon, with renewed strikes pounding the country. Protesters in Tel Aviv swarmed the US embassy demanding an end to the wars. The widening conflict is driving a global oil crisis, with Asia particularly exposed and cities like Cairo already dimming their lights as energy prices soar.

sources.title: Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar

world.headline: Russia Jails Kursk Governor; Child Pulled from Ukraine Rubble
world.body:
Russia sentenced the former governor of the Kursk region to prison in a corruption probe linked to Ukraine's cross-border incursion into the area last year. The case signals Moscow's effort to assign blame for the military embarrassment of Ukraine's surprise offensive on its own soil.

Meanwhile, Russia ramped up strikes on Ukrainian cities, with rescuers pulling a child from rubble in the aftermath. The escalation comes as the war grinds on with no ceasefire in sight.

sources.title: Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine

world.headline: Artemis II Breaks Distance Record; India Police Face Death Penalty
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II mission broke the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth, marking a milestone in the push to return astronauts to deep space.

In India, nine police officers were sentenced to death for killing detainees in custody during the Covid-19 pandemic lockdowns - a rare and severe verdict that drew wide attention. The US Supreme Court also cleared the way for the dismissal of Steve Bannon's criminal contempt case, a significant legal development for the Trump ally.

sources.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth

ai.headline: OpenAI Insiders Revolt Against Altman as Iran Threatens Stargate
ai.body:
Trust in Sam Altman has collapsed among OpenAI's own staff, with insiders describing the CEO as the core problem inside the company. The discontent comes as a group of OpenAI alumni have quietly assembled a fund of up to $100 million to back AI startups independently - a signal that talent is voting with its feet.

Meanwhile, Iran has threatened OpenAI's Stargate data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, raising the geopolitical stakes around the $500 billion infrastructure project. OpenAI separately published a policy vision this week calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI's economic disruption - an ambitious agenda for a company whose internal credibility is under strain.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek

ai.headline: AI Floods iTunes, Rewrites Jobs Data, and Dictates Offline
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, despite being entirely fictional. The takeover is a concrete example of what economists and policymakers have struggled to measure - MIT Technology Review argues this week that granular time-use data, not employment figures, is the only metric that can reveal how AI is actually reshaping work.

On the product side, Google quietly shipped an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, competing directly with tools like Ghost Pepper, a local hold-to-talk speech-to-text app that surfaced on Hacker News. The offline push reflects growing demand for AI that doesn't route sensitive audio through the cloud.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS

ai.headline: Vibe Coding Backlash Grows as Agent Infrastructure Matures
ai.body:
A pointed essay circulating on Hacker News argues that "vibe coding" - shipping AI-generated code without understanding it - is dogfooding taken to a dangerous extreme. The critique lands as the tooling around AI agents grows more serious: Freestyle launched sandboxed environments built specifically for coding agents, and Reducto released Deep Extract, an agent designed to parse complex documents at scale.

A biologically inspired memory system called Hippo also appeared, aiming to give AI agents more structured recall modeled on the hippocampus. The gap between enthusiast hype and production-grade infrastructure is narrowing fast, which makes the debate over developer judgment more consequential, not less.

sources.title: The cult of vibe coding is dogfooding run amok
Launch HN: Freestyle – Sandboxes for Coding Agents
Reducto releases Deep Extract
Show HN: Hippo, biologically inspired memory for AI agents

ai.headline: Trump Science Cuts Deepen as macOS Hides a 49-Day Time Bomb
ai.body:
The White House's latest budget proposal again targets deep cuts to federal science funding, continuing a pattern that researchers warn will erode US competitiveness in AI and other critical fields. The cuts arrive as NASA's Artemis hardware - the rocket and capsule - is reportedly performing well, though the lunar lander program remains the weak link in the return-to-moon timeline.

On the software side, developers discovered a quiet disaster in macOS: TCP networking silently breaks after 49.7 days of uptime due to a timer overflow bug. Adobe drew separate fire after it emerged the company modifies users' hosts files to detect Creative Cloud installations - a move critics called a covert system modification with no legitimate justification.

sources.title: Trump's next budget once again calls for massive cuts to science
NASA's Moon ship and rocket seem to be working well, so what about the landers?
A macOS bug that causes TCP networking to stop working after 49.7 days
Adobe modifies hosts file to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $70K as Markets Flash Mixed Signals
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed back above $70,000 Monday after a rough stretch, but profit-taking is capping the recovery. Contrarian indicators suggest a potential bottom is forming, yet the options market tells a different story - traders are quietly pricing in significant downside, with put skew rising in ways that rarely show up without follow-through.

Strategy added another 4,871 BTC for $330 million, pushing its holdings near 767,000 BTC despite paper losses exceeding $14.5 billion in Q1. Analysts note Strategy is absorbing nearly three times the new supply Bitcoin miners are producing, a dynamic that could push prices toward $110,000 if demand holds. Geopolitics adds another variable - Trump's ultimatum to Iran is injecting fresh uncertainty that could move markets either way.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch

btc.headline: Polymarket Overhauls Exchange, Ditches Bridged Stablecoin
btc.body:
Polymarket is replacing USDC.e, its bridged stablecoin, with native USDC as part of a broader exchange overhaul. The upgrade gives the prediction market platform direct control over its own order matching and settlement infrastructure - a significant shift away from reliance on third-party systems.

The move comes as Polymarket pushes to sharpen its competitive edge and reduce counterparty risk. Separately, a US appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down rival prediction market Kalshi's sports betting operations, handing the federally regulated platform a major legal win. The ruling reinforces CFTC jurisdiction over prediction markets and weakens state-level attempts to treat them as illegal gambling.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

btc.headline: Dimon Warns JPMorgan Must Move Faster on Tokenization
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as genuine competitive threats, naming them alongside fintech and tech giants as forces reshaping finance. He acknowledged JPMorgan's own Kinexys blockchain platform but said the bank needs to accelerate. The candor is notable from a CEO who spent years dismissing crypto.

The comments land as tokenization gains real institutional traction. Bernstein analysts argued this week that Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double the company's stock price by unlocking loan growth at scale. The convergence of traditional finance urgency and tokenization infrastructure suggests the sector is moving from pilot projects to core business strategy.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance

btc.headline: Crypto Regulation, Security, and Governance All Flare at Once
btc.body:
US Senate Banking Committee member Bill Hagerty confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation, adding pressure on Congress to deliver a framework that has stalled for years. Movement on that front would reshape how exchanges, tokens, and intermediaries operate across the board.

On the security side, a new AI-powered cybercrime tool is targeting crypto and banking KYC systems using deepfakes, raising alarms about identity verification gaps. Meanwhile, Aave lost Chaos Labs, one of its primary risk managers, amid a broader contributor exodus and governance disputes - a sign that decentralized protocol management remains fragile under pressure. Samson Mow also warned that rushing a quantum computing fix for Bitcoin could introduce new vulnerabilities rather than eliminate existing ones.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
Rushed quantum fix may backfire for Bitcoin, Samson Mow warns

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate the cod for at least 24 hours — the longer fermentation time breaks down proteins and creates that signature melt-in-the-mouth texture most home cooks skip

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or dumbbells (moderate weight), a clear 20-metre corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for timed sets, building grip strength, core stability, and posture after a weekend of likely sitting

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memory, and selfhood, spawning centuries of debate from Hobbes to Parfit.
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' argues personal identity is less determinate than we assume, which quietly dismantles how most people think about self-improvement, regret, and responsibility — unsettling and genuinely useful on a Monday
